issue_title: Why am I getting "Endpoint request timed out" at the end instead of returned jason?
The disaggregation matrix should be 5-dimensional, not 6-dimensional
issue_body:
Right now eeach task builds a matrix of shape (d1, d2, d3, d4, d5, d6), where d6 is the number of tectonic region types. Since all sources in a task are of the same tectonic region type, the matrix is full of
zeros except for the index corresponding to the given tectonic region type. In a model with 9 tectonic region types (i.e. India) we are essentially using 9 times more memory than needed. Now, this is not
necessarily a problem for the memory, since normally the memory occupation is not dominated by the size of the matrix. Still, it is a problem for the data transfer, since the size of the transferred arrays is proportional to the number of tectonic region types. In this case we would be transferring 9 times more data than needed, all space wasted in the transfer of zeros.

issue_title: Easier-to-understand naming for LSTMBuilder
issue_body:
LSTMBuilder currently represents a relatively unorthodox implementation of LSTMs using coupled input and forget gates: http://dynet.readthedocs.io/en/latest/unorthodox.html#lstm-implementation

I think very few people will realize this, and this could have a significant impact on results (for example, the DyNet LSTMBuilder will never have a cell state with abs(c)>1, but vanilla LSTMs can). It'd probably be best to have the basic LSTMBuilder represent something that looks more like what people expect, so I propose the following change:

* Rename LSTMBuilder to CoupledLSTMBuilder
* Make LSTMBuilder a typedef for VanillaLSTMBuilder

If there aren't any objections, maybe we'll do this in the upcoming 1.1 release: https://github.com/clab/dynet/projects

issue_title: Applications with many web workers frequently fail healthchecks
issue_body:
I've been trying to setup healthchecks on an application with 12 web workers.  Almost every time I try to add the `HEALTHCHECK_URL` config, it fails because the healthcheck fails.  I've been visiting the healthcheck URL while this process is going on, and it has always reported success.

I looked into the deis-controller logs, and it seems to be failing like this:

    WARNING app: failed to connect to container ('Key not found : /deis/services/app/app.web.2')

We've been running a development version of this app with healthchecks for a couple of months without issue.  The main difference between the two versions is the number of workers they have - the production version having a lot more than the development version.

Could this be because the publisher is waiting for `healthcheck_initial_delay` seconds for each of the containers it's publishing in series, but the controller is only waiting once?  So if many of the containers end up on the same machine then the publisher will still be waiting for initial delays by the time the controller starts running it's healthchecks.

issue_title: Error in upgrading-deis.rst
issue_body:
In the docs this is the procedure to do a manual update:

> $ ssh core@<server ip>
> $ sudo su
> $ echo GROUP=stable > /etc/coreos/update.conf
> $ systemctl unmask update-engine.service
> $ systemctl start update-engine.service
> $ update_engine_client -update
> $ systemctl stop update-engine.service
> $ systemctl mask update-engine.service
> $ reboot

this is wrong (at least now).
Why? https://github.com/coreos/bugs/issues/262

the boot partition is updated after the reboot so if the update-engine service is disabled it will not run and update the boot flags.
Only after this output from `journalctl -u update-engine.service`
`[0226/232543:INFO:update_attempter.cc(674)] Updating boot flags...`
is safe to disable the update-engine service. (after the next update it will use the updated version)

This means that https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/contrib/coreos/user-data.example#L25 must be remove so after the reboot with the update update-engine.service will start automatically

reference https://github.com/coreos/bugs/issues/262

issue_title: [revamp] setuptools "wronng ELF class" issues
issue_body:
I'm trying to write a `setuptools` Recipe (as part of getting Twisted working), and currently have:

```python
from pythonforandroid.toolchain import PythonRecipe, shprint
from os.path import exists, join
import sh


class SetuptoolsRecipe(PythonRecipe):
    version = '18.3.1'
    url = 'https://pypi.python.org/packages/source/s/setuptools/setuptools-{version}.tar.gz'

    depends = ['python2']

    def prebuild_arch(self, arch):
        super(SetuptoolsRecipe, self).prebuild_arch(arch)
        build_dir = self.get_build_dir(arch.arch)
        if exists(join(build_dir, '.patched')):
            print('setuptools already patched, skipping')
            return
        self.apply_patch('fix-setup-remove-io.patch')
        shprint(sh.touch, join(build_dir, '.patched'))

    def build_arch(self, arch):
        # build setuptools for android
        super(SetuptoolsRecipe, self).build_arch(arch)
        # build setuptools for python-for-android
        #self.install_python_package()
        # trying to replicate this line from old py4a recipe:
        # try $BUILD_hostpython/hostpython setup.py install -O2 --root=$BUILD_hostpython --install-lib=Lib/site-packages

recipe = SetuptoolsRecipe()
```

```diff
diff -u setuptools-18.3.1.orig/setup.py setuptools-18.3.1/setup.py
--- setuptools-18.3.1.orig/setup.py     2015-09-07 02:55:31.000000000 +0000
+++ setuptools-18.3.1/setup.py  2015-09-12 02:12:50.880759164 +0000
@@ -3,7 +3,6 @@
 Distutils setup file, used to install or test 'setuptools'
 """

-import io
 import os
 import sys
 import textwrap
@@ -48,9 +47,7 @@

 console_scripts = list(_gen_console_scripts())

-readme_file = io.open('README.txt', encoding='utf-8')
-
-with readme_file:
+with open('README.txt', encoding='utf-8') as readme_file:
     long_description = readme_file.read()

 package_data = {
```

The patch is to get around a "wrong ELF class" error with `io` (similar to kivy/plyer#128), but now I get the same error with `ctypes` (FYI I had to modify `sh.py` to output the traceback):

```
[INFO]:    Building setuptools for armeabi
name is None <class 'pythonforandroid.recipes.setuptools.SetuptoolsRecipe'>
[INFO]:    setuptools apparently isn't already in site-packages
[INFO]:    Installing setuptools into site-packages
[INFO]:    -> directory context /home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/other_builds/setuptools/armeabi/setuptools
[INFO]:    running python.host setup.py install -O2
           working ... ImportError: /home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/python-installs/twisted/lib/python2.7 ...Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "setup.py", line 20, in <module>
    exec(init_file.read(), command_ns)
  File "<string>", line 11, in <module>
  File "/home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/other_builds/setuptools/armeabi/setuptools/setuptools/__init__.py", line 12, in <module>
    from setuptools.extension import Extension
  File "/home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/other_builds/setuptools/armeabi/setuptools/setuptools/extension.py", line 8, in <module>
    from .dist import _get_unpatched
  File "/home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/other_builds/setuptools/armeabi/setuptools/setuptools/dist.py", line 18, in <module>
    from setuptools import windows_support
  File "/home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/other_builds/setuptools/armeabi/setuptools/setuptools/windows_support.py", line 2, in <module>
    import ctypes
  File "/home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/python-installs/twisted/lib/python2.7/ctypes/__init__.py", line 10, in <module>
    from _ctypes import Union, Structure, Array
ImportError: /home/str4d/.local/share/python-for-android/build/python-installs/twisted/lib/python2.7/lib-dynload/_ctypes.so: wrong ELF class: ELFCLASS32
```

I'm obviously doing something wrong, but the `setuptools` recipe for the old `python-for-android` didn't have any special library handling, which makes me think that there is some difference in the revamp causing it. It's a bug somewhere, I just don't know if it's in `toolchain.py` or my understanding of how to write recipes :P

issue_title: Running `pipenv --completion` is very slow
issue_body:
Running `pipenv --completion` is consistently slow (it takes about 1 second to run), even after the command has been run multiple times and everything has been loaded into memory.

This means including `eval $(pipenv --completion)` in a `.bashrc` file makes the user wait for a second until the shell prompt comes up.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS: Kubuntu 17.04
1. Python version: 2.7.13
1. Pipenv version: 9.0.1

My shell is bash.

##### Expected result

The command shouldn't take so long to run.

##### Actual result

When running in bash, the command takes a whole second to run.

```
$ time pipenv --completion

_pipenv_completion() {
    local IFS=$'\t'
    COMPREPLY=( $( env COMP_WORDS="${COMP_WORDS[*]}" \
                   COMP_CWORD=$COMP_CWORD \
                   _PIPENV_COMPLETE=complete-bash $1 ) )
    return 0
}

complete -F _pipenv_completion -o default pipenv


real    0m0.987s
user    0m0.888s
sys     0m0.080s
```

##### Steps to replicate

Run `time pipenv --completion` in a bash shell.

P.S. This is a great project.

issue_title: Shebang length exceeded in pip executable
issue_body:
When a new virtualenv is created, there are a number of things installed into that virtualenv.  One of these things is the python package installer, pip.  

On Linux, the pip executable is a shell script.  At the top of this pip shell script is a shebang (#!) line that specifies the python interpreter in the virtualenv.  This line contains the absolute path to the python interpreter.  

If the absolute path to the python interpreter in the virtualenv is very long (deeply nested and/or large path names), it may exceed the maximum length allowed for a shebang line.

The maximum length for a shebang line is limited in the kernel by BINPRM_BUF_SIZE, set in /usr/include/linux/binfmts.h.  On the Linux machines I looked at, this limit is set to 128.

So, when the path, to where a python virtualenv is created, becomes too long, using that virtualenv's pip will not work. It will fail with the error: "bad interpreter: No such file or directory"

A work-around for this problem is to not run pip directly, but by run the virtualenv's python interpreter and pass the pip script to python as the source to execute.

issue_title: [App Service] [Discussion] Shorten appservice command group name to just "app"
issue_body:
I'd love to discuss whether we could shorten the `appservice` command group to just `app` (or equivalent)  in order to improve the ergonomics around frequent use. The use of `az` instead of "Azure" feels so fluid to type (as does nsg, vm, nic, acs, acr, etc.), and I wonder whether we couldn't apply a similar naming abbreviation "principal" for App Service, in order to improve the efficiency of using its command on a regular basis, while balancing intuitiveness/discoverability.

Personally, I don't find the use of <TAB> for auto-completing long command names to be very ergonomic, which is why I've thrown out the possibility of shortening names vs. relying on tab completion. Others may find this acceptable, so I'm just providing one input, which may not be that useful. Apologies if this conversation has already happened. I just wanted to throw it out there while the App Service module was still in beta, and could potentially make a breaking change like this.

**UPDATE:** As discussed below, an alternative solution to this could be to "flatten" out the `appservice` command group and provide top-level support for web apps itself (e.g. `az webapp`). I personally prefer this solution, and would support it over the above previous proposal

issue_title: [DNS] 'az network dns record-set cname set-record' should be '... update' instead
issue_body:
The DNS 'SOA' and 'CNAME' record types are similar, in that they don't support multiple records in the same record set. For that reason, they can't use the same method as the other record types to update the existing record (for the existing types, we use 'add-record' to add a new record followed by 'remove-record' to remove the old one...that doesn't work since the record set must support 2 records at the same time.)

For 'SOA' we have an 'update' command. For 'CNAME' we have a 'set-record' command. But basically they do the same thing--update the existing record.

For consistency, the 'set-record' command for CNAME should be renamed as 'update'.

issue_title: Add write permission to objects only if necessary
issue_body:
Currently, when an object is edited, the `write` permission is given to the creator/editor.

This way, there's always someone with `write` permission.

Problem, revoking permission on children is hard  (#144).

One idea could be to add the `write` permission only if the user has no `write` permission on the parent.

Examples:
- `write` on bucket → create sub collection → no write on sub collection
- `write` on bucket → update sub collection → no write on sub collection
- `read` and `collection:create` on bucket → create collection → write added on collection
- `read` and `record:create` on collection → create record → write added on record

Permissions specified via settings should be taken into account with this approach. Related https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/issues/892

issue_title: kinto CLI command to move a collection from one bucket to another.
issue_body:
The thing is to make sure to create all the record from the lower `last_modified` record to the greatest one so that we keep the exact same `last_modified` value for the records.

The syntax could be:

```
   kinto move-collection /buckets/fennec/collections/experiments /buckets/fennec-staging/collections/experiments
```

- [ ] Fail early if the destination collection already exists
- [ ] The collection data and permissions should be recreated
- [ ] If collection or record permission are set to group the group should be created if they doesn't exist or ignore if they do exist.
- [ ] All the records should be created keeping their original permissions
- [ ] An option --no-permission can be set to only copy the records without keeping the permissions.

issue_title: Update parent last_modified ?
issue_body:
Currently, bucket and collections are resources, exactly like records. When a bucket or collection is deleted, every underlying objects are deleted. But when an underlying object is modified the parent timestamp (`last_modified`) is not bumped.

For example, when querying `GET /buckets/blocklists/collections`:
```
GET /buckets/blocklists/collections
{"data":[{"last_modified":1447944869447,"id":"certificates"}]}
```

Here, the `last_modified` of the collection objects are only modified when the collection itself is changed (for example, when a `schema` is set, or a `cache_expires`, or any collection attribute).

It may be relevant to update the timestamp of the collection when one of its records is created/changed/deleted. Same for buckets (with underlying collections and groups). 

This would ease global polling for changes for example. But nonetheless, there would be no difference between a timestamp bump for a collection attribute change or for an underlying record change.

If we don't want this, we should at least clarify it in the documentation.

issue_title: Circular dependency in Parsl
issue_body:
Reported by @dfj604 

The following code will hang due to a circular dependency : 
```
@App('python', dfk)
def App_A (x):
    import time
    time.sleep(0.2)
    return x*2

@App('python', dfk)
def AppSum (inputs=[]):
    return sum(inputs)

# Creating a list of App_Futures
app_futures = [ App_A(i) for i in range(0,2) ]
# Sum depends on the future results from the previous step
app_futures.extend([AppSum(inputs=app_futures)])
# However by extending app_futures with the future from AppSum, AppSum
# now depends on itself and will block forever.

print(app_futures)
[i.result() for i in app_futures]
```

While this maybe not ideal code from the user, this is something parsl should try to protect against.
Making a deep copy of the args and kwargs to immutable containers (say tuples) could help.

issue_body:
```
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier
from sklearn.datasets import load_boston
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

num_classes = 3
t = scikit_data.target
target = np.digitize(t, np.histogram(t, bins = num_classes - 1)[1]) - 1

coreml_model = skl_converter.convert(scikit_model, scikit_data.feature_names)

issue_title: Mongo support on Windows
issue_body:
All indications are that MongoDB support on Windows has been broken and not used for some time.  If this is mistaken, we would love to hear about your usecase.

Due to support overhead and the incurred development inertia, my preference for dealing with mongo on windows given current development resources is the following:

* Disable mongo on windows, skipping the test suite, etc.
* Document the lack of support on windows appropriately
* Solicit end users who need mongo on windows to make their usecase known.

I propose disabling mongo support on windows for the full 0.4.1 release in odo and the next bugfix release of blaze.

@sandhujasmine I believe you have some familiarity with mongo and blaze: what's your experience here, specifically w.r.t. windows?

issue_title: Conda should be able to create environments for other platforms (than its own)
issue_body:
ATM `conda create` creates environments based on the platform of the conda installation (i.e. what is displayed as platform if you call `conda info`. Usually that is all one ever needs, because what use is an environment if it can not run on your system. There are IMHO exactly two exceptions to that: 
 1) win-64 systems can run win-32 binaries (I'm not certain if this only applies to Windows)
 2) if one uses conda to create custom "(portable) python installations" that are to be shipped to other systems. 
With regards to case 1): Most systems nowadays are 64bit (at least the Windows ones), unfortunately there are still quite a lot of legacy software products that are 32bit, which by means that any interaction with them from python requires a 32bit python.

While one certainly can install conda 64 and 32 parallel and then create the desired environment with the matching conda, this is IHMO kind of unsatisfying, as one has two (mini)conda installations to maintain and only one can be active (i.e. first on PATH) at a time.

I propose adding flag to `conda create` that allows specifying the target platform. This would also require to separate/subdivide the `/pkgs` folder according to platform.

issue_title: Exporting conda environments - can the channel information be included?
issue_body:
In constructing the conda environments I use, I install packages from various channels at anaconda.org. When I export those environments using `conda env export`, the resulting `.yml` file doesn't include the channel information.

For instance, it will look like this:
```
name: my-environment
dependencies:
- appnope=0.1.0=py27_0
- backports_abc=0.4=py27_0
- biggus=0.13.0=py27_0
- ...
```
when it should look like this:
```
name: my-environment
channels:
- ioos
dependencies:
- appnope=0.1.0=py27_0
- backports_abc=0.4=py27_0
- biggus=0.13.0=py27_0
- ...
```
I then have to manually edit the file to add the channel information before uploading the environment to anaconda.org. This isn't such a big deal, but for the uninitiated it might cause errors that they can't figure out how to fix (i.e. it isn't immediately obvious that you'd need to manually edit an exported `.yml` file).

issue_title: Conda create gives many sha256-mismatch error messages
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x ] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior conda create gives numerous error messages "The package for wheel located at ... appears to be corrupted... has a sha256 mismatch." These errors seem to apply to setuptools and many others. It happens on more than one machine. SafetyError: The package for wheel located at /opt/anaconda/miniconda3/pkgs/wheel-0.30.0-py35hd3883cf_1 appears to be corrupted. The path 'lib/python3.5/site-packages/wheel/__pycache__/__init__.cpython-35.pyc' has a sha256 mismatch. reported sha256: c44a94bf1b089749a267419527242ac3909fd802ad0b86e9737c0708bbb60b70 actual sha256: 2ffece4ac40285bd48a28a7df87f4614752463ee5cc8ae5f43b111c50581aa20 SafetyError: The package for wheel located at /opt/anaconda/miniconda3/pkgs/wheel-0.30.0-py35hd3883cf_1 appears to be corrupted. The path 'lib/python3.5/site-packages/wheel/__pycache__/__main__.cpython-35.pyc' has a sha256 mismatch. reported sha256: d0233ab26a33707e0ca5fe2952d7e328def59b82a07789a05e3196321b4f10a0 actual sha256: 8fa56b47a339bf8726885efc0c093c96d3b9f3b5127641dae6f1732de88775ff SafetyError: The package for wheel located at /opt/anaconda/miniconda3/pkgs/wheel-0.30.0-py35hd3883cf_1 appears to be corrupted. The path 'lib/python3.5/site-packages/wheel/__pycache__/archive.cpython-35.pyc' has a sha256 mismatch. reported sha256: fa9ddf881c91e92e61b04e4f2539abad2508bd220116c1261a354aaf4d688876 actual sha256: d7f1df3d2b1690f69dd57c409dd29e7cd42a2f108d61d76e4585164ff65b8ec4 ... and many more ### Steps to Reproduce ``` conda create -n xxx python=3.5 ``` ## Expected Behavior Should create the environment with python 3.5 and no warnings. ## Environment Information
`conda info`

``` active environment : None user config file : /home/abc/.condarc populated config files : conda version : 4.4.7 conda-build version : not installed python version : 3.6.3.final.0 base environment : /opt/anaconda/miniconda3 (writable) channel URLs : https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/main/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/main/noarch https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/noarch https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/r/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/r/noarch https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/noarch package cache : /opt/anaconda/miniconda3/pkgs /home/abc/.conda/pkgs envs directories : /opt/anaconda/miniconda3/envs /home/abc/.conda/envs platform : linux-64 user-agent : conda/4.4.7 requests/2.18.4 CPython/3.6.3 Linux/4.4.0-98-generic ubuntu/16.04 glibc/2.23 UID:GID : 1001:1001 netrc file : None offline mode : False ```

`conda config --show-sources`

``` (no output) ```

`conda list --show-channel-urls`

``` # packages in environment at /opt/anaconda/miniconda3: # asn1crypto 0.23.0 py36h4639342_0 defaults ca-certificates 2017.08.26 h1d4fec5_0 defaults certifi 2017.11.5 py36hf29ccca_0 defaults cffi 1.11.2 py36h2825082_0 defaults chardet 3.0.4 py36h0f667ec_1 defaults conda 4.4.7 py36_0 defaults conda-env 2.6.0 h36134e3_1 defaults cryptography 2.1.4 py36hd09be54_0 defaults docopt 0.6.2 hdfs 2.1.0 idna 2.6 py36h82fb2a8_1 defaults libedit 3.1 heed3624_0 defaults libffi 3.2.1 hd88cf55_4 defaults libgcc-ng 7.2.0 h7cc24e2_2 defaults libstdcxx-ng 7.2.0 h7a57d05_2 defaults ncurses 6.0 h9df7e31_2 defaults openssl 1.0.2n hb7f436b_0 defaults pip 9.0.1 py36h6c6f9ce_4 defaults pycosat 0.6.3 py36h0a5515d_0 defaults pycparser 2.18 py36hf9f622e_1 defaults pyopenssl 17.5.0 py36h20ba746_0 defaults pysocks 1.6.7 py36hd97a5b1_1 defaults python 3.6.3 h6c0c0dc_5 defaults readline 7.0 ha6073c6_4 defaults requests 2.18.4 py36he2e5f8d_1 defaults ruamel_yaml 0.11.14 py36ha2fb22d_2 defaults setuptools 36.5.0 py36he42e2e1_0 defaults six 1.11.0 py36h372c433_1 defaults sqlite 3.20.1 hb898158_2 defaults tk 8.6.7 hc745277_3 defaults urllib3 1.22 py36hbe7ace6_0 defaults wheel 0.30.0 py36hfd4bba0_1 defaults xz 5.2.3 h55aa19d_2 defaults yaml 0.1.7 had09818_2 defaults zlib 1.2.11 ha838bed_2 defaults ```

issue_title: TypeError when trying to upload environment
issue_body:
I get the following error when trying to upload an environment:

```
conda env upload --file text.yml 
Uploading environment test to anaconda-server (https://api.anaconda.org)... 
An unexpected error has occurred, please consider sending the
following traceback to the conda GitHub issue tracker at:

    https://github.com/conda/conda/issues

Include the output of the command 'conda info' in your report.


Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/johannes/.local/opt/miniconda3/bin/conda-env", line 6, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/home/johannes/.local/opt/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda_env/cli/main.py", line 68, in main
    return args_func(args, parser)
  File "/home/johannes/.local/opt/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 202, in args_func
    args.func(args, p)
  File "/home/johannes/.local/opt/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda_env/cli/main_upload.py", line 101, in execute
    info = uploader.upload()
  File "/home/johannes/.local/opt/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda_env/utils/uploader.py", line 84, in upload
    distribution_type=ENVIRONMENT_TYPE, attrs=self.env_data)
  File "/home/johannes/.local/opt/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/binstar_client/__init__.py", line 412, in upload
    _hexmd5, b64md5, size = compute_hash(fd, size=size)
  File "/home/johannes/.local/opt/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/binstar_client/utils/__init__.py", line 259, in compute_hash
    hash_obj.update(s)
TypeError: Unicode-objects must be encoded before hashing
```

Thanks!

issue_title: Conda update working differently
issue_body:
If a user installs the latest Anaconda 5.1 and then they try to update anaconda-client:

```
conda install anaconda=5.1.0
conda update anaconda-client 
```

Conda will say everything is up to date and will not attempt to update to 1.6.11 from 1.6.9.

This issue seems to be another manifestation of it:
- https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues/issues/8603
- https://github.com/ContinuumIO/navigator/issues/1437

Seems to be related with the Anaconda metapackage and how its handling changed from  4.3.x?

issue_title: virtualenv created with conda virtualenvpackage incorrectly uses system python
issue_body:
It looks like the virtualenv package is pretty broken, so:

```
tweedledee:~ chris$ which python
/Users/chris/anaconda/bin/python
tweedledee:~ chris$ python
Python 2.7.9 |Anaconda 2.1.0 (x86_64)| (default, Dec 15 2014, 10:37:34) 
[GCC 4.2.1 (Apple Inc. build 5577)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
Anaconda is brought to you by Continuum Analytics.
Please check out: http://continuum.io/thanks and https://binstar.org
>>> 
tweedledee:~ chris$ which virtualenv
/Users/chris/anaconda/bin/virtualenv
tweedledee:~ chris$ conda list | grep virtual
virtualenv                13.0.1                   py27_0  
```

Now try and create a virtualenv:

```
tweedledee:~ chris$ virtualenv ~/virtualenvs/testve
New python executable in /Users/chris/virtualenvs/testve/bin/python
Installing setuptools, pip, wheel...done.
tweedledee:~ chris$ /Users/chris/virtualenvs/testve/bin/python
Python 2.7.6 (default, Sep  9 2014, 15:04:36) 
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 6.0 (clang-600.0.39)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> 
```

...and find its been created with the system python.

That's not right, I suspect the fix will need to be in the conda virtualenv package...

issue_title: Deprecate 'conda env attach' and 'conda env upload'
issue_body:
These two commands are outside the scope and spirit of conda.  The first requires knowledge of a "notebook," which is not one of conda's fundamental units of work.  The second requires knowledge of the Anaconda API, which is the purview of anaconda-client, not conda.

Deprecation warnings will be given in conda 4.4, with the command scheduled for removal in conda 4.5.

I would expect 1) the tutorial on https://docs.continuum.io/anaconda-cloud/using to work as described, 2) the software to not require a token or login to get the a public conda package, 3) this error message to recognize that I am indeed logged in with 'anaconda login' and give a better message, and maybe  4) the message's advice in (b) message to indicate how to get a valid token.

I'm on a Mac OSX10.10.5, and I've been having what seems to be similar updating problems with anaconda for a while, as seen in https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/3399#issuecomment-257362542 , https://github.com/Anaconda-Platform/docs.anaconda.org/issues/14 , and https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1233#issuecomment-257379052

issue_title: source activate shouldn't completely replace PS1
issue_body: I don't think we want to completely replace `PS1` with `source activate`.  It violates expectations based on previous conda-env and virtualenv behavior.  It also makes me think I'm in a subprocess--like a python shell or ssh-ing somewhere--where `ctrl-d` is the natural exit.  I've hit `ctrl-d` several times with testing as a natural reflex, when what I really wanted to do was `source deactivate`.

issue_title: Refactor TabularCPD
issue_body:
Currently `TabularCPD` has the these attributes: `variable`, `variable_card`, `evidence`, `evidence_card`.
And since it inherits `Factor`, it also has: `variables`, `cardinality` and `values`. 

`evidence` and `evidence_card` can be easily derived if we know other attributes. And since we are not directly using `evidence` or `evidence_card` too many times, we can remove these two and compute these when needed. Right now `evidence` and `evidence_card` is adding overhead in a lot of operations because we need to update these as well as `variables` and `cardinality`.

issue_title: Cannot create an object with a custom naming behavior
issue_body:
Because of these lines:

```
        derived_id = id or title
        new_id = chooser.chooseName(derived_id, content)
```
in https://github.com/plone/plone.api/blob/d65c81e6eeb69f0a581fa50e9f0500372efe45b5/src/plone/api/content.py#L115

I will never reach line 34 (`nameFromTitle = INameFromTitle(obj, None)`) here
 https://github.com/plone/plone.app.content/blob/9c220b2ceace9c74a464529d89e4a66c16c0fc17/plone/app/content/namechooser.py#L34

For this reason it is not possible to create with `plone.api` an object that authomatically gets the id from a custom `INameFromTitle` behavior.

I noticed this with a custom `INameFromTitle` adapter that gives an id based on the date, but I think this is an issue also for the `File` content type (which uses this behavior: https://github.com/plone/plone.app.dexterity/blob/184184150ab6b7743769fe92bf8ec78e5fb52873/plone/app/dexterity/behaviors/filename.py#L16)

issue_title: Numpy floating point precision causes SpaCy vocab KeyValue error
issue_body:
If you have a `token` in your `nlp(text)` whose `token.orth` is a very large int (for example: `886050111519832510`), then in `WMD._common_vocabulary_batch`, `numpy.concatenate((words1, words2))` will approximate those integers in `joint, index = numpy.unique(numpy.concatenate((words1, words2)), return_index=True)`, thereby returning incorrect/invalid vocabulary keys for `evec = self.embeddings[joint]` in `WMD._estimate_WMD_relaxation_batch`.


```python
ipdb> 886050111519832510 in words1
True
ipdb> 886050111519832510 in words2
True
ipdb> 886050111519832510 in numpy.concatenate((words1, words2))
False
```

This last false occurs because 886050111519832510 has been rounded to 886050111519832448 as a numpy.float.

A hack solution is to use strings, or something more immutable.
```python
    def _common_vocabulary_batch(self, words1, weights1, i2):
        words2, weights2 = self._get_vocabulary(i2)
        words1_np = numpy.array(words1, dtype=numpy.str)
        words2_np = numpy.array(words2, dtype=numpy.str)
        joint, index = numpy.unique(numpy.concatenate((words1_np, words2_np)),
                                    return_index=True)
        nw1 = numpy.zeros(len(joint), dtype=numpy.float32)
        cmp = index < len(words1)
        nw1[numpy.nonzero(cmp)] = weights1[index[cmp]]
        nw2 = numpy.zeros(len(joint), dtype=numpy.float32)
        nw2[numpy.searchsorted(joint, words2)] = weights2
        return joint, nw1, nw2
```

Then convert back to int when querying for the appropriate SpaCy vectors.
```python
# Hook in WMD
class SpacyEmbeddings(object):
    def __getitem__(self, item):
        return nlp.vocab[int(item)].vector
```

issue_title: Inconsistent use of np.random.seed
issue_body: Both of `ConstRandnShift` class and `Rotation` class call `np.random.seed`. If seed is not given in the constructors,  the former doesn't call `np.random.seed`, but the latter calls it without an argument, which sets the seed using `/dev/urandom`, therefore we loose the full control of the random numbers. For a bug fixing, I prefer to have full control of random numbers. It is easy to correct, but I am not sure if it is a feature or a bug.

issue_title: \n added to query in sparqlstore.py

These lines cause a spurious '\n' to be inserted in the query string:

https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/blob/master/rdflib/plugins/stores/sparqlstore.py#L77-L81

Example:
```
from rdflib.plugins.stores import sparqlstore

store = sparqlstore.SPARQLStore()
query = "SELECT DISTINCT * WHERE {?s ?p ?o} LIMIT 10"

store.setQuery(query)
print repr(store.queryString)
```

This prints ```'\nSELECT DISTINCT * WHERE {?s ?p ?o} LIMIT 10'```

I am sorry you are having problems - I went through all the wiki-pages a little while ago to make sure they were still current, and it worked fine for me.

)

I have released a rdfextras 1.0 - if you re-run easy_install you should be ok!

Add parameters initNs and initBindigs in rdflib.graph.query()

def query(..., initNs={}, initBindings={})

return result(processor.query(query_object, initBindings, initNs))

issue_title: For gluon, how to define NET (MODEL) and LOSS for multi_labels?
issue_body:
This maybe a complex question about applicaiton of mxnet.  And I really got stuck for a loooog time. I hope someone could help me out, thanks very much~

**target:**
I want to transfer[ the multi_label net](https://github.com/xlvector/learning-dl/blob/master/mxnet/ocr/cnn_ocr.py) to gluon version. The similar example with R is [here](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/example/captcha/mxnet_captcha.R).The core idea is to learn to predict 4 labels in a net. l And I have 2 problems with mxnet 0.12 on win10.

First, accordingly, to define net with gluon, I wrote with hybridBlock like:
```
class Net(gluon.HybridBlock):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
       super(Net, self).__init__(**kwargs)

        with self.name_scope():
            self.cov1 = gluon.nn.Conv2D(channels=32, kernel_size=(5, 5))
            self.cov2 = gluon.nn.Conv2D(channels=32, kernel_size=(5, 5))
            self.cov3 = gluon.nn.Conv2D(channels=32, kernel_size=(3, 3))

            self.max_pool = gluon.nn.MaxPool2D(pool_size=(2, 2), strides=(1, 1))
            self.avg_pool = gluon.nn.AvgPool2D(pool_size=(2, 2), strides=(1, 1))

            self.flatten = gluon.nn.Flatten()
            self.dense_256 = gluon.nn.Dense(256)
            self.dense_10 = gluon.nn.Dense(10)

    def hybrid_forward(self, F, x):
        x = F.relu(self.max_pool(self.cov1(x)))
        x = F.relu(self.max_pool(self.cov2(x)))
        for _ in range(2):
            x = F.relu(self.avg_pool(self.cov3(x)))

        x = self.flatten(x)
        x = F.relu(self.dense_256(x))
        x1 = self.dense_10(x)  # for 10 nums
        x2 = self.dense_10(x)
        x3 = self.dense_10(x)
        x4 = self.dense_10(x)
        out1 = F.concat(*[x1, x2, x3, x4], dim=0)
        return out1

        # return x1
        # maybe no need to use the following  3 statements in gluon
        # label = mx.symbol.transpose(data = label)
        # label = mx.symbol.Reshape(data = label, target_shape = (0,))
        # return mx.symbol.SoftmaxOutput(data=fc2, label=label, name="softmax")
```

About the net:
**Q1; As I didn't find any example to modify label when defining net in hybrid class,  I guess it is correct to just end with `out1 = F.concat(*[x1, x2, x3, x4], dim=0)`?**

Then, when defining the 'trainer' (gluon version), the core code is like:
```
for i, batch in enumerate(train_iter):  # data shape = BYXC, label shape = (batchsize,)
    # get batch info and store data label on GPU
    data, label, batch_size = get_batch(batch, ctx)
    with autograd.record():
        output = net(data)  # data shape must be BCYX, output shape = (batchsize*Label_NUM,SOFTMAX_NUM)
        label = label.T.reshape((-1,)) # before: label shape = (batchsize*Label_NUM,)
        L = losser(output, label) 
    L.backward()
    trainer.step(batch_size)

def get_batch(batch, ctx):
    """return data and label on ctx"""
    '''the batch shape should be BCYX'''
    if isinstance(batch, list):  # for gluon.data.DataLoader
        data, label = batch
    else:  # for (subclass) of mx.io.DataBatch
        data, label = batch.data[0], batch.label[0]

    return (data.as_in_context(ctx), label.as_in_context(ctx), data.shape[0])  # data.shape[0] = batch_size
    # if no use try_all_gpu(),TypeError: object of type 'Context' has no len()
    # return (gluon.utils.split_and_load(data, ctx),
    #         gluon.utils.split_and_load(label, ctx),
    #         data.shape[0])
```
And here with the losser(loss function), it is defined as `gluon.loss.SoftmaxCrossEntropyLoss()`:
**Q2: As it would not be treated as correct unless all 4 labels in 1 example are matched,then is `  label = label.T.reshape((-1,))` essential and correct  here to calculate  loss?**  by the way, even for the referred old version, I still didn't understand why the label need to transpose and flat to a long vector.

issue_title: How to insert constant multiplier layer in my model definition
issue_body:
Hi,

From MXNet documentation, I follow the following strategy to define my model:

```python
data = mx.sym.Variable('data')
net = mx.sym.FullyConnected(data, name = 'fc1', num_hidden = 128)
net = mx.sym.Activation(net, name = 'relu1', act_type = "relu")
net = mx.sym.FullyConnected(net, name = 'fc2', num_hidden = 10)
net = mx.sym.SoftmaxOutput(net, name = 'softmax')
model = mx.mod.Module(net)

model.bind(data_shapes = nd_iter.provide_data, label_shapes = nd_iter.provide_label)
model.init_params()  
model.fit(nd_iter, num_epoch = 10)
```
where `nd_iter` is my MXNet *data iterator*.

Now let's say I want to insert a layer that multiplies all the values in that layer by a *constant* value (something achieved using `lambda` layers in *Keras*). From what I have understood till now, this can be achieved by inserting a *broadcasting* layer:

```python
multiplier = mx.symbol.Variable('constant', shape = (1,))
data = mx.sym.Variable('data')

net = mx.sym.FullyConnected(data, name = 'fc1', num_hidden = 128)
net = mx.sym.Activation(net, name = 'relu1', act_type = "relu")
net = mx.sym.broadcast_mul(lhs = net, rhs = multiplier, name = 'multiply') # new layer
net = mx.sym.FullyConnected(net, name = 'fc2', num_hidden = 10)
net = mx.sym.SoftmaxOutput(net, name = 'softmax')
model = mx.mod.Module(net)
```

My first question is, is this the proper (and most elegant) way to do it?
If yes, how do I assign value to the `multiplier` (let's say 10 for example)?

I understand that *values* can be assigned to *symbols* using
 `multiplier.bind(ctx = mx.cpu(),args = {'constant':10})`.

But how does this go into the entire model definition? 

Thanks in advance.

## Minimum reproducible example

import mxnet as mx
import numpy as np

vocab_size = 1000
embedding_size = 50

word_vecs = np.random.uniform(-1.0, 1.0, (vocab_size+1, embedding_size))

nd_sample_data = mx.nd.array(x)
nd_word_vecs = mx.nd.array(word_vecs)

embedding_data = mx.sym.Variable('embedding_data')
embeddings = mx.sym.Embedding(data=embedding_data, 
                              input_dim=vocab_size+1, # Add 1 for padding
                              output_dim=embedding_size,
                              name="embedding")
print(embeddings.list_arguments(), embeddings.list_outputs())
print(embeddings.infer_shape(embedding_data=nd_sample_data.shape, 
                       embedding_weight=word_vecs.shape))

([(5L,), (1001L, 50L)], [(5L, 50L)], [])

inputs = {"embedding_data": nd_sample_data, 
          "embedding_weight": nd_word_vecs}
executor = embeddings.bind(ctx=mx.cpu(),
                           args=inputs)
executor.forward()
============ Kernel crashes at this point ============

Any clues ?

issue_title: assimp-config.cmake sets incorrect ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR when library path is deeply nested.
issue_body:
This may or may not prove related to #1912 but when ASSIMP_LIB_INSTALL_DIR contains more than one directory level the logic below isn't sufficient to find the root directory producing a broken CMake configuration.

https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/620182770da9f851a44040af69cb20ba6c3f6106/assimp-config.cmake.in#L13-L16

This affects the libassimp-dev package on Debian Stretch, Buster, and Sid and Ubuntu Artful and Bionic. Verifiable on these platforms with the following example CMakeLists.txt:
```cmake
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.0)
project(asitest)

find_package(assimp)

message(STATUS "ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR: ${ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR}")
message(STATUS "ASSIMP_LIBRARY_DIRS: ${ASSIMP_LIBRARY_DIRS}")
message(STATUS "ASSIMP_INCLUDE_DIRS: ${ASSIMP_INCLUDE_DIRS}")
```

Selected output from above CMakeLists.txt:
```
...
-- ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR: /usr/lib
-- ASSIMP_LIBRARY_DIRS: /usr/lib/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu
-- ASSIMP_INCLUDE_DIRS: /usr/lib/include
...
```

issue_title: Accepted Unit Testing patterns fail to store result
issue_body:
Please let me know if I am missing something.

Celery tasks should generally be set to ALWAYS_EAGER for unit testing
http://docs.celeryproject.org/en/latest/userguide/testing.html?highlight=unit%20test

I noticed that when publishing my new Aspnet Core 1.1 (just updated to VS 2017) project it is always missing the forwardWindowsAuthToken="true" from the web.config.

More info and the fix I found was here:

https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/content/problem/26751/publish-aspnet-core-to-iis-with-windows-authentica.html

I also tried adding:

services.Configure<IISOptions>(options => { options.ForwardWindowsAuthentication = true; });

to my ConfigureServices method but it didn't help.

So my question is, is there a way to get this to work properly? Some command line argument or other way to automate it other than having to manually edit the web.config after it has been deployed?

I don't think it matters, but this is a asp.net core 1.1 targeting 4.6.2 not core.

http://stackoverflow.com/questions/44057005/aspnet-core-1-1-missing-windows-authentication-when-published?noredirect=1#comment75139041_44057005

Since we have the deploy automated its annoying to have to manually edit the web.config file, is it possible to automate that? Is it a known issue? Our older 1.0 core projects do properly add forwardWindowsAuthToken="true" in the web.config.

issue_body:
Steps I took:

sudo apt-get install python-pip
pip install networkx  # puts networkx in ~/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/
virtualenv --python=python3.6 .venv3
source .venv3/bin/activate
pip install importlab
echo "import networkx" > foo.py
importlab -V2.7 foo.py --tree

`foo.py` shows up as the only file in the tree. On the other hand, if I install importlab under Python 2.7 and analyze Python 3.5 code (I didn't try 3.6 because pip3 is 3.5 on my machine), the last command (correctly) causes a bunch of networkx files to be printed as part of the tree.

issue_title: Core dump using tensorflow and SentencePiece when importing SentencePiece before
issue_body:
This has been reported by a few members of our team. If you use SentencePiece in a project with Tensorflow, there is a crash where the core is dumped if you import tensorflow after SentencePiece. 

An effect of this is that if you are in a script that itself doesn't use tensorflow but imports from modules that import tensorflow, if you import sentencepiece after importing the scripts using tensorflow, the core is dumped.

We are running Tensorflow 1.10.1 with python 2.7.15.

It's very easy to reproduce for us:
```
jules@OctoBrain:~/$ python
Python 2.7.15 |Anaconda custom (64-bit)| (default, May  1 2018, 23:32:55)
[GCC 7.2.0] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import sentencepiece
>>> import tensorflow
Segmentation fault (core dumped)

jules@OctoBrain:~/$ python
Python 2.7.15 |Anaconda custom (64-bit)| (default, May  1 2018, 23:32:55)
[GCC 7.2.0] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import tensorflow
>>> import sentencepiece
>>> # this is fine
```

issue_title: The UMD code fragment does not call AMD's define correctly
是否应该 reraise requests.RequestException 为 WeChatClientException
issue_body:
目前的设计，在调用 ``WeChatClient`` 的相关接口时，不会去捕获 ``requests.RequestException``，而是使用了 ``raise_for_status`` 方法在 API status_code 为非 2xx 的情况下自动 raise ``requests.RequestException``.

https://github.com/jxtech/wechatpy/blob/master/wechatpy/client/base.py#L63

如此导致在调用接口时，不仅要捕获 ``WeChatClientException`` 还需要考虑捕获 ``requests.RequestException``，增加了调用者的负担。

可能的设计是将 ``requests.RequestException`` 捕获并 reraise 为 ``WeChatClientException``，并对 ``WeChatClientException`` 增加 ``request`` 和 ``response`` 属性。参考类似实现：

https://github.com/jxtech/teambition-api/blob/master/teambition/client.py#L137

issue_title: Adding code coverage for base + mpi branch
issue_body:
I was able to automate the build of the mpi branch and add code coverage to the python files. I've been unable to do the same for the cython code. Could someone help me with the same ?  

[Here](https://github.com/s-sajid-ali/pyFFTW/tree/mpi-pr-new) is the branch in which I've built upon @fredRos's mpi branch. The travis builds are [here](https://travis-ci.com/s-sajid-ali/pyFFTW/builds).  I've used [spack](https://github.com/spack/spack/) to build and mpich load fftw (linked to mpich). The codecov reports are [here](https://codecov.io/gh/s-sajid-ali/pyFFTW/branch/mpi-pr-new). They worked until I didn't touch the cython files. 

I then followed [this advice](http://blog.behnel.de/posts/coverage-analysis-for-cython-modules.html) to get the codecov results for the cython code and now I get this error `Fatal Python error: PyThreadState_Get: no current thread`. I've tried to search for what this means and all I've found out is that this is caused by multiple versions of python as per [[1](https://github.com/caffe2/caffe2/issues/854#issuecomment-320865933)] though I don't see how this is happening in the travis build. 

I think this might be caused by the fact that I'm passing a `disutils` flag to the build while the build is using `setuptools`. In that case, there's this advice [here](https://medium.com/@dfdeshom/better-test-coverage-workflow-for-cython-modules-631615eb197a) which I tried to follow but I didn't see a cythonize in the `setup.py`. I'm unsure as to how the compiler directives are to be added. Could someone guide me here ?

issue_title: %sossave magic to make full use of sos command line power.
issue_body:
I am not quite happy with the `%runcell` and `%run` magics. The first magic execute the current cell as a workflow and the second magic execute the entire notebook (cells with section header). It seems more logical to give a larger operation a longer name, so perhaps we should give them names `%run` and `%sosrun`.

More importantly, the notebook interface does not take full advantage of the sos command line system, namely subcommands such as `sos remove`, `sos pack`, `sos unpack` etc. We could assign additional magics but it makes more sense to use these features as 

```
%sossave myworkflow.sos
!sos run myworkflow workflow parameter...
!sos remove -s
```

Basically, `%sossave` saves the current notebook as a sos script (taking only the cells with section header) so that we can execute it with commands such as `sos run`.

Race Condition in zerocopy `send()`
issue_body:
It looks like there is a race condition in zerocopy sends where closing the parent context in another thread can lead to a very confusing traceback.  

Functionally this is pretty unlikely, but it happens to us roughtly daily b/c we allocate/detallocate contexts something like a million times a day.

     self._sock.send_multipart(parts, flags=zmq.NOBLOCK, copy=False)
   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/zmq/sugar/socket.py", line 368, in send_multipart
     return self.send(msg_parts[-1], flags, copy=copy, track=track)

issue_title: Windows encoding of __init__.py breaks setup with certain locales
issue_body:
The ```setup.py``` script parses the version number from the ```adal/__init__.py```. This can fail with certain locales on Linux when Python3 is being used:

```bash
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python> LANG=C python3 setup.py build
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "setup.py", line 35, in <module>
    open('adal/__init__.py').read()).group(1)
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.6/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
    return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xef in position 0: ordinal not in range(128)
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python>
```

This is because of the Windows encoding of ```__init__.py``` contains a byte-order mark at the beginning of the file:

```
<U+FEFF>#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

simply converting ```__init__.py``` to standard Unix format fixes the problem:

```bash
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python> dos2unix adal/__init__.py 
dos2unix: converting file adal/__init__.py to Unix format...
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python> LANG=C python3 setup.py build
running build
running build_py
creating build
creating build/lib
creating build/lib/adal
copying adal/constants.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/adal_error.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/cache_driver.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/mex.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/xmlutil.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/authority.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/authentication_context.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/wstrust_response.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/self_signed_jwt.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/authentication_parameters.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/util.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/argument.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/user_realm.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/oauth2_client.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/token_cache.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/log.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/wstrust_request.py -> build/lib/adal
copying adal/token_request.py -> build/lib/adal

copying adal/code_request.py -> build/lib/adal
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python>
```

This problem also affects the building of RPM packages. In fact, I stumbled over this issue when trying to build the python3 RPM package for ADAL.

Please consider plain UTF-8 for the source code encoding.

issue_title: Functions in user schema for obs augmentation
issue_body:
Hi @andy-esch @stuartlynn 

I came across the [following functions](https://github.com/CartoDB/cartoframes/blob/product-code-review/cartoframes/assets/data_obs_augment.sql) that are installed in the user schema for DO augmentation purposes.

First of all, why are we creating this functions? Couldn't just we write the SQL directly in the SQL API request? Also `obs_data_table` seems to be unused.

And another question. Could we use Batch API for this? SQL API has short timeouts, and will have problems augmenting large datasets. On the other hand, Batch API is harder to use (polling). I guess this depends a bit on the use case, maybe this idea doesn't make much sense for notebooks, since you don't expect to be waiting for minutes for your answer anyway.

issue_title: Discussion: should we have coverage on tests?
issue_body:
My argument is yes we should. Testing is very important and doubly so that all the test code gets written. Consider the following test:
```python
def test_iter():
    a  = db.restream(hdr)
    for b in a:
        assert something
```
This test will not fail if a is empty, we will simply miss the assert. One way to deal with this is to throw a known fail into the code, run it, check that it failed, then remove it because it is messy. Another way to deal with this would be to run coverage on the test code. Coverage would show that the for loop line was ran but none of the internals were executed thus hinting to us that the internal asserts are not being tested.

One potential solution is to run coverage twice, one without the test code (for our line tallys) and one with the tests covered to test the health of the test codebase.

issue_title: Fully support Python 3.4.
issue_body:
There are several closed issues which resolve with (usually @dan-blanchard saying):

> You just need to install the Python 3 compatible fork of fabric in our [requirements.txt](https://github.com/Parsely/streamparse/blob/08ef3d461243c6dc906f8773c84dc0140200a395/requirements.txt#L2).

https://github.com/Parsely/streamparse/issues/86#issuecomment-101348677 is currently the most useful doc for this. Even the [FAQ](http://streamparse.readthedocs.org/en/master/faq.html#is-streamparse-compatible-with-python-3) from the docs says:

> Yes, streamparse is fully compatible with Python 3 starting with version 3.3 which we use in our [unit tests](https://github.com/Parsely/streamparse/blob/master/.travis.yml).

A short-term fix would be to make the docs clearer about the `requirements.txt` usage. A longer-term fix is to drop Fabric altogether, as discussed in #45 where #99 is relevant.

issue_title: Specify virtualenv command-line arguments
issue_body: Someone pointed out that if you run streamparse on old environments (e.g. old CentOS versions), there might be a global install of Python 2.6 that virtualenv tries to use by default. You'd like to be able to override this with a Python binary using `virtualenv -p`. This is even required if you want the virtualenv to use pypy rather than Python, for example.

issue_title: Add a way to add dependent files into the model archive
issue_body:
We are definitely going to need this eventually.  The vocabulary already does this, but in a particular, hard-coded way.  ELMo embeddings would probably be easier if there were a standard way to do this.  And if we ever want to do byte pair encoding, e.g., you'd want to do it as a `Tokenizer` that takes a pre-computed byte-pair vocabulary (and this is separate from the way that our `Vocabulary` works).

So, we'd want something on `Trainer` that saves all dependent files to the model archive.  That would then call a method on `Vocabulary`, the `DatasetReader`, the `Model`, and anything else, which in turn call the method on `Tokenizers`, `Modules`, and whatever else.  And when loading a model from an archive, these classes all need to know how to modify the paths that they require as input, so they point to the archive somehow instead of pointing to the originally-specific location.  Maybe `update_params_from_archive(params: Params, archive_path: str)`, or something?  That method would update the params for anything that matters - e.g., `Embedding` would remove the path to the pre-trained file, because it's not necessary anymore, `Vocabulary` would add a path to the saved files, etc.

This is a lot of boilerplate, adding methods on `Model` to pass on calls to `Modules`, etc., but I'm not sure of another way to do this.

issue_title: Adding a project that already exists (with the --monitor flag) indicates a change happened
issue_body: If a project already exists and I issue a `tower-cli project create` command with `--name` targeting that project and the `--monitor` flag set too, the output is yellow. This is indicating that a change was made, but there was no change made. For all other resources, if I do this, the output is green, indicating that no change has occurred because the default force_on_exists is False.

issue_title: Translate local file strings to AWS::Include transform
issue_body:
There are quite a few issues on this repo related to variable substitution in separate YAML files, particularly dealing with `x-amazon-apigateway-integration`'s `uri` parameter.

What if, instead of `aws cloudformation package` translating a local file string to an `s3://` URI, it translated it to an AWS::Include transform?

This SAM / CloudFormation template:

```yaml
Resources:
  Api:
    Properties:
      DefinitionUri: ./swagger.yaml
```

… currently translates to:

```yaml
Resources:
  Api:
    Properties:
      DefinitionUri: s3://bucket/swagger.yaml
```

: but could instead translate to:

```yaml
Resources:
  Api:
    Properties:
      DefinitionUri:
        Fn::Transform:
          Name: AWS::Include
          Parameters:
            Location: s3://bucket/swagger.yaml
```

issue_title: ADFS auth cache is stored improperly
issue_body:
The adfs_auth value in the credentials file is base64-encoded, but it is not done using URL-safe Base64, which means that the value spans multiple lines.  Because the other lines aren't like key=value, this causes problems in the Java AWS SDK, which aggressively validates the file as it loads it.

I believe the problem is here:

https://github.com/byu-oit/awslogin/blob/9c5a42e8aaa0d53473e02ade3a6bc3c77c00b01c/byu_awslogin/util/data_cache.py#L26

I would just hand you a PR, but I don't know enough about the python standard lib to fix this.

## My operating system is (include version):

```
# cat /etc/*-release
CentOS release 6.7 (Final)

CentOS release 6.7 (Final)
```

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):

```
# wget https://dl.eff.org/certbot-auto
```

## I ran this command and it produced this output:

```
# ./certbot-auto --non-interactive --agree-tos --email steve@example.com
...
Creating virtual environment...
Installing Python packages...
Installation succeeded.
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log
Plugins selected: Authenticator apache, Installer apache
Missing command line flag or config entry for this setting:
Which names would you like to activate HTTPS for?
example.com? www.example.com

(You can set this with the --domains flag)

IMPORTANT NOTES:
 - Your account credentials have been saved in your Certbot
   configuration directory at /etc/letsencrypt. You should make a
   secure backup of this folder now. This configuration directory will
   also contain certificates and private keys obtained by Certbot so
   making regular backups of this folder is ideal.
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

I used non-interactive mode but it still prompted for me to specify domains (understandable, param was missing).  I think it's good that it throws this error, however there's no flag for "select all" like there is in the interactive version.

I'd like something like `--domains ALL` or `--domains-all`, etc.

I'm writing a script that will be run on about 20 servers to get certificates.  I'd rather not have to specify domains for each.  I could grep apache files and parse them out, but since Certbot is already doing this, there should be an option to get certificates for everything it can find when non-interactive.

TL;DR: non-interactive mode needs a way to create certs for all domains founds without the need to specify actual names.

issue_title: Apache default default
issue_body:
So in `certbot_apache/constants.py` we have this code:
```
    os_info = util.get_os_info()
    try:
        constants = CLI_DEFAULTS[os_info[0].lower()]
    except KeyError:
        constants = CLI_DEFAULTS["debian"]
```
Basically, if you're OS isn't one we we expect, we assume it's Debian. Is this the correct behavior? With Debian, we assume there are `sites-available`/`sites-enabled`, `mods-available`/`mods-enabled`/, `a2enmod`/`a2dismod`, etc.

Perhaps if we can't recognize your OS, we should choose defaults with `handle_mods` and `handle_sites` set to `False` by default. I created this issue in response to #3302.

@joohoi, IIRC you wrote most of this code. What do you think?

issue_title: Please provide examples of renew hook RENEWED_LINEAGE and RENEWED_DOMAINS values
issue_body:
Thanks for all your work on Let's Encrypt, it's fantastic!

I wrote a renew hook that would only be triggered by a certain domain. Renew hooks are a little tricky to get right since they get called infrequently (only at renewal time, usually every couple of months).

I assumed the lineage directory (`RENEWED_LINEAGE`) was `/etc/letsencrypt/live`, but it's really `/etc/letsencrypt/live/$domain`.

It would be awesome if the CLI help for `--renew-hook` had example values for these environment variables. It would give hook authors more confidence that they've got their code right.

Perhaps something like this:

```
the shell variable $RENEWED_LINEAGE will point to the config live subdirectory
(for example, "/etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com") containing the new certs and
keys; the shell variable $RENEWED_DOMAINS will contain a space-delimited list
of renewed cert domains (for example, "example.com www.example.com").
```

issue_title: iterating over Template misses nodes
issue_body:
Surprisingly calling `get_node_instances(t.template, IfNode)` and `get_node_instances(t.template.nodelist, IfNode)` are not equivalent. The latter is more complete and behaves to expectation. The former does not in that it misses all top level nodes.

issue_title: Traitlet API
issue_body:
Since matlplotlib's folks are starting to write traitlet-based APIs, we probably need to think of a roadmap for the future of the library if it is to be more widely adopted by the Scipy community. We discussed this with @jasongrout on the way back to NYC yesterday.

There are some improvements that we could easily make without separating traitlets into a two different repos, for example

- deprecate trait attribute declaration with TraitType types instead of trait type instances

```Python
class Foo(HasTraits):
    bar = Int    # deprecated
    baz = Int()  # ok
    alpha = List(trait=Int)     # deprecated
    alpha = List(trait=Int())   # ok
```

- like in [atom](https://github.com/nucleic/atom), separate the metadata from the keyword arguments in TraitType's constructor

```Python
x = Int(allow_none=True, sync=True)           # deprecated
x = Int(allow_none=True).metadata(sync=True)  # ok
``` 

- having a `once` version of the `on_trait_change`. 
This is quite cumbersome to make with the current on_trait_change implementation. 

- more generally start discussions on a new method to replace `on_trait_change` in the future, with a more convenient signature and a simpler name.

I will be adding things to this list, but this is a start.

issue_title: Question regarding approach to authentication, alternative suggestion
issue_body:
I am running Jupyter internally to provide an IPython notebook environment to multiple users. I have looked into the security model provided by Jupyter Hub, and I'm not 100% comfortable with it. It seems to involve running the core process as root, then relying on the server to sudo into userspace to execute the notebooks. Having the server running as root seems inherently open to vulnerability.

I was looking for a simpler username/password challenge based on a simple lookup, with the server running using a low-privilege service role account. It seems like that might provide a stronger sandbox.

It seems to be this could be implement by modifying (or supplying an alternative) LoginHandler within login.py within the auth module. An alternative LoginHandler could then use a simple file lookup containing hashed passwords, or even offer email signup style functionality. I was wondering whether anyone had considered this approach, and what their thinking was in terms of the current implementation?

A simple-ish extension could allow for the hub to load a user/password dictionary whose filename was supplied in the config file, storing hashed passwords based on a configured salt value. This would have to be manually maintained by the server admin, but this would at least give me some greater sense of security at running code inside my intranet. It would be enough for me for now...

issue_title: can't create, commit to detached HEAD, merge
issue_body:
Various websites, and the changelog, suggest that pygit2 lets you set a detached HEAD with something like `repo.head = repo[commit_id]`. If that ever actually worked, it doesn't any more (0.22.0), because you get `"AttributeError: attribute 'head' of '_pygit2.Repository' objects is not writable"`.

(Apologies if this bug report actually reflects a problem with libgit2 rather than pygit2, but I'm unsure where the boundary between the two projects is.)

## Why this is a problem (what I’m trying to achieve)

Committing to detached HEADs is proscribed by most Git experts, but I think it's necessary in order to correctly implement editing a Git repository through a web app. When the web form that edits a particular file loads, it needs to know the id of the current commit (i.e. the commit which the version of the file being edited comes from). When the changes are made and submitted, what the web app should do is check out the commit that the edit was based on, apply the change, check out the branch again and merge it in. This way you correctly generate a merge conflict when someone comes along and makes an edit at the same time as you, and avoid clobbering edits. You effectively re-create the repository state as it was when the user started editing before doing anything.

Note that the messages `git checkout` gives when you checkout a commit, or make changes on a detached HEAD then switch back to the original branch, suggest that this is impossible, and that the only way to save commits made against a detached HEAD is to create a new named branch then merge that in. This is false: it's perfectly possible, and sensible, to use an 'anonymous branch' for this and directly merge the commit you 'left behind' into the main branch using the commit id. (And, if nothing else happened to the branch since the branch you checked out was created)

## Trying to do this with pygit2: A comedy of frustration

As mentioned above, despite it being documented that pygit2's API sensibly represents detached HEADs by returning a Commit rather than Reference object from `Repository.head`, and semi-documented that you should be able to *make* the HEAD detached by setting this property, this doesn't work.

### Other things that don't work

#### Creating a commit with an old parent directly

This would actually be my preferred solution to this sorry mess (it's actually the first thing I tried before thinking of using a detached HEAD), since it would work on a bare repo without having to make an unnecessary working tree. The error message here is `GitError: failed to create commit: current tip is not the first parent`.

#### Checking out on a bare repo

Okay, I didn't *expect* this to work, but I did kind of hope that since the whole idea of 'checking out' doesn't really make sense for a bare repo anyway, there might be some magic that made the bare repo look like a repo with a working directory for programmatic purposes. Nope. `"GitError: Cannot checkout. This operation is not allowed against bare repositories."`

Passing a `directory` argument to the checkout function makes the error go away, but then:

#### `repo.checkout_treeish(commit)`

Doing this does indeed change the working tree to reflect the commit. But it doesn't also update `repo.head` to point to the newly-checked-out commit.

issue_title: Update SDRClassifier docstrings for API docs
Remove stripUnlearnedColumns from compute in SPRegion
issue_body:
Finishes replacing outdated issue #1262 (followup to issue #2278)

```stripUnlearnedColumns``` does not need to be default behavior within compute. This will only affect the case of a spacial pooler region being run with learning mode disabled AFTER learning has occurred. 

After ```stripUnlearnedColumns``` is removed, the default behavior for this case will be for active, unlearned columns to remain in the output of the spacial pooler if learning is disabled. This does not affect the spacial pooler if learning is enabled or if it is being with only it's randomly initialized values (no learning enabled).

issue_title: TM initialization parameters should be accessible via getter methods
issue_body:
Just like the SP, the TM should expose the initialization parameters sent to its constructor through getter methods. This allows users to programmatically inspect the TM object to see how it was created. This is a list of initialization parameters that are not exposed.


### `maxSegmentsPerCell`
This param is used to create an instance of the `Connections` class, which exposes `maxSegmentsPerCell` as a public property. So it is possible to get this value from the TM with `tm.connections.maxSegmentsPerCell`, but a getter would make the TM API more direct.

### `maxSynapsesPerSegment`
This param is used to create an instance of the `Connections` class, which exposes `maxSynapsesPerSegment` as a public property. So it is possible to get this value from the TM with `tm.connections.maxSynapsesPerSegment`, but a getter would make the TM API more direct.

issue_title: Expose control over Django bulk_save option
issue_body:
I'm running into an issue with Django's QuerySet.bulk_create() on multi-table inheritance which [might be addressed in some cases](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/28821). The use of this feature is controlled using the bulk_save option on [pyexcel_io.database.importers.django.DjangoModelWriter](https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-io/blob/a9bbbc4de7afcde289d745264d5bb115bbc0b8f1/pyexcel_io/database/importers/django.py#L20)...however, this is not exposed by pyexcel_io.database.importers.django.DjangoBookWriter:

>             sheet_writer = DjangoModelWriter(
>                self.__importer, model,
>                batch_size=self._keywords.get('batch_size', None))

A simple patch to address this would be to change that to:

>             sheet_writer = DjangoModelWriter(
>                self.__importer, model,
>                batch_size=self._keywords.get('batch_size', None),
>                bulk_save=self._keywords.get('bulk_save', None))

or (unless there is some reason to limit what is passed?) possibly even:

>             sheet_writer = DjangoModelWriter(
>                self.__importer, model, **self._keywords)

Higher up, the ability to specify bulk_save is present in pyexcel.internal.core.save_sheet and save_book but is not exposed by pyexcel.internal.meta.BookMeta.save_to_django_models. This would need to be trivially addressed too.

FWIW, I'm presently using pyexcel-io 0.5.4, but I see that master (as above) also currently has this issue.

issue_title: BUG: The torchvision.models.alexnet is incorrect
issue_body:
In [self.classifier](https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/master/torchvision/models/alexnet.py#L32), the correct order of layers should be fc6-relu-dropout-fc7-relu-dropout-fc8, which means that dropout layer should be after the fc and relu layers.

This does not matter when inference. However, when you fine tune AlexNet model on other datasets, the incorrect layer sequence will result in an accuracy drop of 2 percents or more.

Can I make a PR to fix it? The pre-trained model URL need to update too.

issue_title: _find_classes() underperforming for large numbers of classes
issue_body: When training with large (>10^5) numbers of classes (Ref. https://arxiv.org/abs/1805.01978) the _find_classes() method is sloooow. Replacing the first line with: `classes = [d.name for d in os.scandir(dir) if d.is_dir()]` works correctly for me and is a lot faster (see Python docs: https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.listdir ). Thought others might appreciate this...

issue_title: Trigger run method twice for tasks yields some dynamic dependencies
issue_body:
Whenever a task yields a dynamic dependency, it's run method triggered twice. Here is a case to produce it;


```python
from unittest import TestCase

from luigi.scheduler import Scheduler
from luigi.worker import Worker

from tests.helpers import DummyTask

_author_ = 'ahmetdal'


class TriggeringRunTwiceCase(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.sch = Scheduler()

    def test_with_dynamic_dependencies(self):
        outputs = []

        class B(DummyTask):
            def run(self):
                super(B, self).run()
                outputs.append('B Run')

        b = B()

        class A(DummyTask):
            def run(self):
                super(A, self).run()
                outputs.append('Before yield B')
                yield b
                outputs.append('After yield B')

        a = A()

        with Worker(scheduler=self.sch) as w:
            self.assertTrue(w.add(a))
            self.assertTrue(w.run())

            expected_outputs = [
                "Before yield B",
                "B Run",
                "After yield B",
            ]

            self.assertListEqual(expected_outputs, outputs)
```


Output of this test;

```console
Second list contains 1 additional elements.
First extra element 3:
'After yield B'

- ['Before yield B', 'B Run', 'After yield B']
+ ['Before yield B', 'B Run', 'Before yield B', 'After yield B']
?                            ++++++++++++++++++


```


As you can see, the part before the yield is running twice (not the part after the yield.). `'Before yield B'` output seems more than one.

issue_title: HdfsAtomicWritePipe silently fails when target file exists
issue_body:
Lately due to some pipeline issues I've ended up re-running a lot of jobs while their output exists. They seem to run fine but the output doesn't change or even get an updated modified time. I tracked this down to the atomic write pipe just silently failing when the move does nothing. I use the snakebite client with hdfs fallback. I believe this is happening in the snakebite client.

Demonstration code:

```
import luigi.contrib.hdfs

target = luigi.contrib.hdfs.HdfsTarget('/tmp/test.txt')
with target.open('w') as fobj:
    fobj.write('test1')

try:
    with target.open('w') as fobj:
        fobj.write('test2')
finally:
    with target.open() as fobj:
        print '\ncontents:', fobj.read()
    target.remove()
```
I would expect to either see `test2` printed. At the very least, I'd expect to see an error message if it prints `test1`, as this means the second write didn't work. Instead I see `test1` and no error message. So it looks like I successfully wrote `test2` to hdfs when I didn't.

issue_title: input_file_parser should automatically sort variants if they aren't sorted.
issue_body:
We already sort all variants (by MAF) in the QQ plot, so sorting is not too expensive.

Sorting all variants in the association files would be easy.  If I were willing to give up on streaming, I could just put `sorted(variants, key=...)` in `.get_variants()`. For `get-cpra`, that will avoid variants with insufficient MAF.  For `augment`, I think I need to sort all variants so that I can merge with `sites.tsv`.  That's unfortunate, but in most cases shouldn't be too bad.

I'd like to use streaming UNLESS they're not sorted.  So, `.get_variants()` asserts sort order.  If it fails, it raises `NotSorted`.  The caller catches that and starts over with `.get_variants(sort=True)`.

issue_title: JSON serialize error on flask.Response objects
issue_body:
### Description

JSON serialize error on flask.Response objects

### Expected bahaviour

From the [documentation](https://connexion.readthedocs.io/en/latest/response.html#response-serialization):

> "If the endpoint returns a Response object this response will be used as is."

### Actual behaviour

The response is parsed in connexion.decorators.decorator.get_full_response() and the [response object itself is mistakenly set as the response's data instead of response.data](https://github.com/zalando/connexion/blob/master/connexion/decorators/decorator.py#L27):

This later throws a JSON serialize error when trying to [JSON serialize the data variable](https://github.com/zalando/connexion/blob/master/connexion/decorators/response.py#L47) (mistaken Response object)

### Steps to reproduce



### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- `python --version`
Python 2.7.12
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"`
Version: 1.0.129

issue_title: redo README
feature request / inconsistent behavior
issue_body:
Hi All,

I really like `Z3_optimize_to_string` function, as it drops the entire problem in SMT-lib compatible way, so that it can be directly loaded into the solver.
Recently I have discovered that `Z3_solver_to_string` behaves in a slightly different fashion: unlike `Z3_optimize_to_string` it does not dump the entire problem: namely, it does not print definitions and sorts of the participating variables.

I agree that this information is technically redundant, but to be able to get a full problem with one API call makes a tremendous difference in convenience.
Thus I would be glad if `Z3_solver_to_string` would also output the whole problem, including variables sorts.
It would also make it more consistent.
